pundit.name: Landon Donovan
pundit.take: **I think "Bubba G" and "Ghost Pepper" might be engaging in a contest of who can draft the most bizarre team.** Look, I get why people say "Bubba G" has a flair for the dramatic—France and Senegal are solid, but when you're relying on Canada to win points in a World Cup pool, I'm left wondering if Gunner is confusing the tournament with a curling championship. Then there's "Ghost Pepper," Devin, bless his heart, struggling at the bottom with zero points after drafting Haiti—what conversation were you having with yourself, Devin? But hey, maybe Bosnia can pull him to a respectable loss here. I've got an unshakable belief in Canada, despite all logical sense, to ice Bosnia in this matchup—because why not trust an untested bunch in a fantasy universe where half the managers barely know the rules?

pundit_takes.headline: Bubba G's Canada faces Ghost Pepper's Bosnia in winless showdown
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gunner's team relies on Canada's signature strategy of hockey-style defense.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe The Noisemaker ties with his dad in world-class standings
pundit_takes.subtitle: Rafe's three non-qualifiers aren't holding him back at all.

pundit_takes.headline: Mustard Boy's Brazil fails to catch up in points race
pundit_takes.subtitle: Turns out painting the locker room yellow isn't enough.

pundit_takes.headline: Backpass Assassin leads standings with not a single upset win
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gayden proclaims victory through unadventurous predictability.

rome_analytics_quotes: Gunner's odds tank as a kid who can't name players messes with the favorite.
Rafe drafted three World Cup dropouts—classic rookie move, pure comedy gold.
Rafe serving Devin that 3-point deficit—who's the real Ghost now, Pepper?
Sim had Rafe competitive? Predicting chaos or just pure sorcery?
